questions.prompt: A company must solve the following requirement: run non-critical batch jobs at the lowest compute price. Which cost-optimized architecture design capability is the BEST fit? The primary focus is security.
questions.hint: Focus on the key requirement: run non-critical batch jobs at the lowest compute price.
questions.explanation: Spot Instances for interruptible workloads is correct because they offer substantial discounts for flexible workloads.
questions.incorrect: The selected option does not best satisfy the stated requirement. Compare each option against the primary constraint in the prompt.

optionExplanations.A: Incorrect. Graviton adoption is useful when you need to improve price-performance for supported workloads on modern ARM-based instances, but it is not the best fit for this requirement.
optionExplanations.B: Correct. Spot Instances for interruptible workloads is the best choice because they offer substantial discounts for flexible workloads.
optionExplanations.C: Incorrect. Scheduled and target tracking scaling is useful when you need to avoid paying for idle compute capacity outside peak periods, but it is not the best fit for this requirement.
optionExplanations.D: Incorrect. S3 lifecycle and tiering policies is useful when you need to move infrequently accessed data to lower-cost storage classes, but it is not the best fit for this requirement.

questions.prompt: A solutions architect is reviewing this need: lower steady-state compute costs with flexible usage commitments. Which option should be implemented first? The primary focus is resilience.

options.text: Cost visibility with Budgets and Cost Explorer

questions.hint: Focus on the key requirement: lower steady-state compute costs with flexible usage commitments.
questions.explanation: Savings Plans commitment strategy is correct because they reduce long-term compute spend compared to On-Demand pricing.
questions.incorrect: The selected option does not best satisfy the stated requirement. Compare each option against the primary constraint in the prompt.

optionExplanations.A: Incorrect. Cost visibility with Budgets and Cost Explorer is useful when you need to detect spending anomalies and enforce proactive cost controls, but it is not the best fit for this requirement.
optionExplanations.B: Incorrect. Graviton adoption is useful when you need to improve price-performance for supported workloads on modern ARM-based instances, but it is not the best fit for this requirement.
optionExplanations.C: Correct. Savings Plans commitment strategy is the best choice because they reduce long-term compute spend compared to On-Demand pricing.
optionExplanations.D: Incorrect. Scheduled and target tracking scaling is useful when you need to avoid paying for idle compute capacity outside peak periods, but it is not the best fit for this requirement.

questions.prompt: During an architecture review, the primary requirement is: move infrequently accessed data to lower-cost storage classes. Which AWS capability is most appropriate? The primary focus is performance.

options.text: Cost visibility with Budgets and Cost Explorer

questions.hint: Focus on the key requirement: move infrequently accessed data to lower-cost storage classes.
questions.explanation: S3 lifecycle and tiering policies is correct because they automate storage cost reduction over the object lifecycle.
questions.incorrect: The selected option does not best satisfy the stated requirement. Compare each option against the primary constraint in the prompt.

optionExplanations.D: Correct. S3 lifecycle and tiering policies is the best choice because they automate storage cost reduction over the object lifecycle.
